healthSummary.summary: Operational source-of-truth has been reset from the example graph. Unity Bridge and Play Mode evidence remain blocked until live Unity connectivity is restored.

nextRecommendedAction.title: Run procedural recovery job E2E validation for seeds 1001, 2026, and 7777.
nextRecommendedAction.reason: Procedural generation must prove deterministic layouts, reachable targets, recovery completion, and zero console errors before further gameplay expansion.

nodes.title: AInvil reliable game production workflow
nodes.summary: AInvil should present a trustworthy requirement-first workflow from user intent to Unity validation evidence without mixing example fixtures into operational state.

nodes.title: Productization cleanup and E2E validation recovery
nodes.summary: Separate examples from operational state, establish canonical Unity Bridge package paths, and expose an executable dry-run E2E status report.

nodes.title: Minimum AInvil E2E happy path
nodes.summary: Track the minimum production workflow: user request, requirement graph, dry-run Unity change, bridge apply, compile check, Play Mode validation, evidence, traceability, dashboard.

nodes.title: Operational state must not depend on example fixtures
nodes.summary: Runtime reports and release readiness must identify example graph, sample harness, and placeholder evidence as non-operational.

nodes.title: Minimum E2E status must be reportable without mutating Unity
nodes.summary: A CLI-accessible report must classify each happy-path step as Verified, Partial, Blocked, Spec-only, or Deprecated/Sample.

nodes.title: Unity Bridge package and input bridge roles must be explicit
nodes.summary: A canonical Unity Bridge package path and preferred runtime input bridge component must be documented and surfaced in productization reports.

nodes.title: AInvil E2E productization status feature spec
nodes.summary: Operational feature spec represented by productization status reports and CLI commands.

nodes.title: Separate example fixtures from operational source of truth
nodes.summary: Copy example graph and sample scenario to examples and reset state/production_state_graph.json to AInvil productization state.

nodes.title: Generate productization status report
nodes.summary: Create JSON and Markdown reports showing feature statuses, E2E step statuses, release blockers, package roles, and input bridge roles.

nodes.title: Expose productization report through CLI
nodes.summary: Add a CLI command that writes and summarizes productization status.

nodes.title: Restore live Unity Bridge connectivity
nodes.summary: Open Unity, import the canonical bridge package, start the bridge server, and rerun doctor/productization checks.

nodes.title: Canonical Unity Bridge package
nodes.summary: Plugin-local package used as canonical install source.

nodes.title: Runtime-safe input validation bridge
nodes.summary: Codex.UnityBridge.AInvilRuntimeInputTestBridge is the preferred runtime validation component; AInvilInputTestBridge remains compatibility/sample surface.

nodes.title: Productization report classifies the E2E path
nodes.summary: Given AInvil is run from the plugin workspace, when the productization report command runs, then each minimum E2E step has a concrete status and next action.

nodes.title: Unity live checks reach compile and Play Mode evidence
nodes.summary: Given Unity Bridge is reachable, when doctor and live harness run, then compile status and Play Mode validation evidence are recorded.

nodes.title: Static productization status report
nodes.summary: Static report generation can run without Unity and records live Unity blockers separately.

evidence.summary: Generated by productization report command; live Unity validation remains separate.

nodes.title: Example fixtures contaminate operational state
nodes.summary: Example graph and top_down_collectible scenario can make AInvil look more validated than it is for a real project.

nodes.title: Restore Unity Bridge connectivity and rerun productization checks.
nodes.summary: Unity Bridge health, compile check, and Play Mode evidence are currently blocked.

nextAction.title: Restore Unity Bridge connectivity and rerun productization checks.
nextAction.reason: The minimum E2E happy path cannot complete until Unity Bridge is reachable.

nodes.title: Dungeon Recovery Company first playable vertical slice
nodes.summary: Generate and validate the first recovery job in a real Unity project without modifying existing game assets.

nodes.title: First Recovery Job
nodes.summary: A player moves through a small dungeon room, recovers Rubble, CorruptionNode, and BrokenRelic, and reaches Job Complete.

nodes.title: Recover three dungeon targets to complete a job
nodes.summary: The generated first playable scene must contain three recoverable targets, movement, recovery interaction, progress UI, and job completion state.

nodes.title: Create isolated first playable Unity assets
nodes.summary: Generate scripts, materials, and scene under Assets/AInvilGenerated/DungeonRecoveryFirstPlayable only.

nodes.title: Register Product MVP live harness scenario
nodes.summary: Create dungeon_recovery_first_playable_e2e as an Operational ProductMvp UnityFeatureE2E scenario.

nodes.title: Validate first recovery job in Play Mode
nodes.summary: Run deterministic validation hooks in Play Mode and export Product MVP evidence.

nodes.title: Three recovery targets exist
nodes.summary: Given the first playable scene is open, when validation inspects the room, then Rubble, CorruptionNode, and BrokenRelic exist as recovery targets.

nodes.title: Recovery progress UI updates
nodes.summary: Given a target is recovered, when the recovery count changes, then HUD progress updates from Recovered: 0 / 3 through Recovered: 3 / 3.

nodes.title: All recoveries complete the job
nodes.summary: Given all three targets are recovered, when the third recovery is applied, then Job Complete is shown and completion state is true.

nodes.title: Recover target input
nodes.summary: WASD/arrows move the player and E recovers the nearest target; deterministic hooks are allowed for E2E evidence.

nodes.title: DRC_FirstRecoveryJob scene
nodes.summary: Generated first playable scene at Assets/AInvilGenerated/DungeonRecoveryFirstPlayable/Scenes/DRC_FirstRecoveryJob.unity.

nodes.title: Dungeon recovery first playable scripts
nodes.summary: Generated runtime scripts and scene builder for recovery target, player controller, UI view, and game controller.

nodes.title: Dungeon Recovery first playable E2E evidence
nodes.summary: Latest Product MVP live harness evidence for dungeon_recovery_first_playable_e2e.

nodes.title: Run Dungeon Recovery Company Product MVP first playable E2E validation.
nodes.summary: Run live harness for dungeon_recovery_first_playable_e2e and promote Product MVP Ready Candidate only if Passed evidence exists.

nodes.title: Procedural Recovery Job
nodes.summary: Seed-based procedural dungeon generation with reachable recovery target placement and deterministic validation hooks.

nodes.title: Seed generates dungeon map
nodes.summary: The procedural recovery job must generate a dungeon map from a configured seed.

nodes.title: Same seed produces same map
nodes.summary: The same seed must produce the same layout signature.

nodes.title: Different seeds produce different maps
nodes.summary: Different tested seeds should not all produce the same layout signature.

nodes.title: Dungeon has rooms and corridors
nodes.summary: Each generated dungeon must contain at least four rooms connected by corridors as one reachable component.

nodes.title: Player starts on valid floor
nodes.summary: The player start must be generated on a valid floor cell.

nodes.title: Three recovery targets are placed on valid floor
nodes.summary: Exactly three recovery targets must be placed on valid non-wall floor cells without duplicate target cells.

nodes.title: All recovery targets are reachable
nodes.summary: All three recovery targets must be reachable from the player start by floor-cell BFS.

nodes.title: Recovering all procedural targets completes the job
nodes.summary: Recovering all three procedural targets must set Job Complete true.

nodes.title: Create procedural dungeon scene
nodes.summary: Create DRC_ProceduralRecoveryJob.unity under generated assets only.

nodes.title: Implement seed-based dungeon generator
nodes.summary: Implement deterministic grid room/corridor generation with fallback behavior.

nodes.title: Implement recovery target random placement
nodes.summary: Place three reachable non-overlapping targets on valid floor cells.

nodes.title: Implement reachability validation
nodes.summary: Validate generated layout with BFS over floor cells.

nodes.title: Implement procedural job UI
nodes.summary: Display seed, recovered count, movement input, and recovery input.

nodes.title: Implement procedural Play Mode validation
nodes.summary: Run seed 1001, 2026, and 7777 through deterministic Play Mode hooks and export evidence.

nodes.title: Seeds generate successfully
nodes.summary: Seeds 1001, 2026, and 7777 generate successfully.

nodes.title: Each seed has at least four rooms
nodes.summary: Each tested seed reports room count >= 4.

nodes.title: Each seed has exactly three targets
nodes.summary: Each tested seed reports recoveryTargetCount == 3.

nodes.title: Each seed has three reachable targets
nodes.summary: Each tested seed reports reachableTargetCount == 3.

nodes.title: No duplicate target cells
nodes.summary: Each tested seed reports duplicateTargetCells == 0.

nodes.title: Procedural recovery reaches Job Complete
nodes.summary: Each tested seed can complete all three targets and reports jobComplete true.

nodes.title: Procedural run has zero console errors
nodes.summary: The procedural validation run reports console error count 0.

nodes.title: No stale evidence reuse
nodes.summary: The procedural evidence records staleEvidenceReused false.

nodes.title: DRC_ProceduralRecoveryJob scene
nodes.summary: Generated procedural recovery job scene.

nodes.title: Procedural recovery job scripts
nodes.summary: Generated procedural controller, player controller, generator, validator, and builder scripts.

nodes.title: Procedural recovery job E2E evidence
nodes.summary: Latest evidence for dungeon_recovery_procedural_recovery_job_e2e.

nodes.title: Run procedural recovery job E2E validation.
nodes.summary: Run live harness for dungeon_recovery_procedural_recovery_job_e2e and keep Public Release Ready as No.

nodes.title: Normal play assigns random startup seed
nodes.summary: Normal play must assign a fresh non-zero random seed at startup and display the active seed in the HUD; fixed seed generation remains available only for validation hooks.

nodes.title: Procedural job uses first-person mouse-look view
nodes.summary: The procedural recovery job must use a first-person camera parented to the player and rotate the view with mouse movement.

nodes.title: Implement random startup seed flow
nodes.summary: Generate a fresh runtime seed on start while retaining deterministic GenerateWithSeed validation hooks.

nodes.title: Implement first-person procedural player camera
nodes.summary: Attach a first-person camera to the procedural player, drive yaw/pitch from mouse movement, and move relative to view direction.

nodes.title: Random startup seed changes between samples
nodes.summary: The operational harness samples GenerateWithRandomSeed twice and records different non-zero seed values.

nodes.title: Procedural playable uses first-person mouse look
nodes.summary: The procedural player controller compiles with first-person camera binding, mouse-look yaw/pitch, and view-relative movement.

nodes.title: Visual screenshots are captured during Play Mode
nodes.summary: The procedural visual gate must capture non-stale PNG screenshots for spawn, mouse look, near target, interaction prompt, and job complete checkpoints.

nodes.title: Camera framing is automatically checked
nodes.summary: The visual gate must detect invalid first-person camera state, camera inside wall, camera outside dungeon, and failed mouse-look or movement checks.

nodes.title: UI and target visibility are automatically checked
nodes.summary: The visual gate must verify progress UI, seed UI, interaction prompt, job complete UI, and near-target visibility.

nodes.title: Missing shader artifacts are automatically checked
nodes.summary: The visual gate must detect suspected missing shaders and high magenta pixel ratio from rendered screenshots.

nodes.title: Implement procedural visual validation probe
nodes.summary: Add a generated visual validation probe under DungeonRecoveryFirstPlayable that captures screenshots and returns camera/UI/material assertions.

nodes.title: Integrate visual gate with AInvil live harness
nodes.summary: Add an operational visual validation scenario, evidence export, review report, and regression --visual support.

nodes.title: Five screenshots are captured
nodes.summary: Visual evidence includes five checkpoint screenshots with valid width and height.

nodes.title: First-person camera framing passes
nodes.summary: Camera exists, is active, is not inside a wall, and is not outside the generated dungeon.

nodes.title: Mouse look and movement are verified
nodes.summary: Visual probe verifies camera pose changes and forward movement changes player position.

nodes.title: UI visibility passes
nodes.summary: Progress, seed, interaction prompt, and Job Complete UI are visible at their checkpoints.

nodes.title: Target visibility passes near target
nodes.summary: At least one recovery target is visible at the near-target checkpoint.

nodes.title: Missing shader checks pass
nodes.summary: Missing shader is not suspected and magenta pixel ratio is below threshold.

nodes.title: Public release remains unclaimed
nodes.summary: Visual validation keeps humanReviewRequired true and publicReleaseReady false.

nodes.title: Procedural visual validation evidence
nodes.summary: Latest evidence for dungeon_recovery_procedural_visual_validation.

nodes.title: Procedural Dungeon Space Quality
nodes.summary: Expand the procedural recovery job from narrow corridor test geometry into wider first-person dungeon rooms with non-blocking primitive props.

nodes.title: Wider first-person rooms
nodes.summary: Rooms must be large enough for first-person exploration, turning, lateral movement, and target search.

nodes.title: Comfortable corridor width
nodes.summary: Corridors must be wide enough to reduce wall snagging and support first-person movement.

nodes.title: Tall dungeon walls
nodes.summary: Walls must be higher than player and camera height to read as dungeon boundaries.

nodes.title: Primitive room props
nodes.summary: Rooms must contain primitive props such as crates, shelves, barrels, pillars, debris, and racks to create exploration structure.

nodes.title: Non-blocking prop placement
nodes.summary: Props must not block player start, doorways/corridors, target cells, target-adjacent interaction space, or target reachability.

nodes.title: Reachable distributed targets
nodes.summary: All three targets must remain reachable and sufficiently separated for each operational validation seed.

nodes.title: Adjust room size range
nodes.summary: Increase procedural room width/depth range and grid size for first-person exploration.

nodes.title: Adjust corridor width
nodes.summary: Carve 3-cell corridors and record corridor width/narrow passage metrics.

nodes.title: Adjust wall height
nodes.summary: Raise procedural wall height and expose wall height sufficiency metrics.

nodes.title: Add primitive prop placement
nodes.summary: Generate room props from Unity primitives while avoiding reserved cells.

nodes.title: Add prop and target clearance validation
nodes.summary: Expose prop overlap, doorway blocking, target blocking, clearance, and reachability metrics.

nodes.title: Run space quality, visual, and build verification
nodes.summary: Validate procedural space quality alongside procedural E2E, visual validation, and build verification.

nodes.title: Seeds generate at least four rooms
nodes.summary: Seeds 1001, 2026, and 7777 each produce room count >= 4.

nodes.title: Average room area increased
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records averageRoomArea >= 49 after room size expansion.

nodes.title: Corridor width sufficient
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records corridorWidth >= 3, narrowPassageCount == 0, and playerClearancePassed == true.

nodes.title: Wall height sufficient
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records wallHeightSufficient == true.

nodes.title: Props are present
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records propCount > 0 and multiple primitive prop types.

nodes.title: Props do not block traversal or targets
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records propOverlapCount == 0, blockedDoorwayCount == 0, blockedTargetCount == 0, and navigabilityAfterPropsPassed == true.

nodes.title: Targets remain reachable
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records reachableTargetCount == 3 and targetReachabilityPassed == true.

nodes.title: Targets have interaction clearance
nodes.summary: Each validation seed records targetInteractionClearancePassed == true.

nodes.title: Job completes on each seed
nodes.summary: Each validation seed reaches jobComplete == true.

nodes.title: Visual validation remains passed
nodes.summary: Procedural visual validation remains Passed after spatial changes.

nodes.title: Build verification remains passed
nodes.summary: Procedural build verification remains Passed after spatial changes.

nodes.title: Procedural space quality latest evidence
nodes.summary: Latest evidence for dungeon_recovery_procedural_space_quality_validation.
